title: Ctenopteris inconspicua
taxonomy: Ctenopteris inconspicua is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family of ferns is commonly known as the wood ferns or dryopteris ferns. The genus Ctenopteris is a small group of ferns native to New Zealand and is closely related to other genera within the Dryopteridaceae family. The classification of Ctenopteris inconspicua is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. Overall, the classification of Ctenopteris inconspicua provides a clear understanding of its evolutionary relationships within the fern family.
parts: Ctenopteris inconspicua is a species of fern, and while there's limited information available on its medicinal properties, the leaves and stems of the plant may be used in traditional medicine. intro: Ctenopteris inconspicua, commonly known as New Zealand lace fern or lace fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. The lace fern is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for soothing skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of the lace fern include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with lace fern include teas and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin care.
